name: Aeneas
description: Aeneas, son of the Trojan prince Anchises and the goddess Venus (Aphrodite), was a Trojan hero who played a prominent role in the Trojan War and is considered the legendary founder of Rome. He was a member of the royal line of Troy and a cousin of King Priam. Throughout the Trojan War, Aeneas distinguished himself as a skilled warrior and leader, often fighting alongside Hector. When Troy fell to the Greeks, Aeneas, guided by divine intervention, led a group of survivors, including his father Anchises, his son Ascanius (also known as Iulus), and the Palladium, the sacred statue of Pallas Athena, away from the burning city. Aeneas's voyage was fraught with perils, including storms, encounters with mythical creatures like the Harpies and Cyclops, and the wrath of the goddess Juno (Hera), who was hostile to the Trojans. He landed in Carthage, where he had a passionate affair with Queen Dido, but was ultimately compelled by the gods to abandon her and continue his journey to Italy. In Italy, Aeneas faced further trials, including war with the Rutulians and their king Turnus, before finally establishing a settlement that would eventually become the foundation of the Roman civilization. Aeneas is revered as the progenitor of the Roman people, embodying virtues such as piety, duty, and leadership. His epic journey, as chronicled in Virgil's Aeneid, became a cornerstone of Roman identity and a powerful narrative of national origin.
subtitle: Founder of Rome, Trojan Hero

keyMyths.title: The Escape from Troy
keyMyths.description: As Troy burned, Aeneas, guided by his mother Venus, fled the city carrying his father Anchises and leading his son Ascanius. He also rescued the Palladium, a sacred statue believed to protect the city.

keyMyths.title: Aeneas and Dido
keyMyths.description: Aeneas landed in Carthage, where he fell in love with Queen Dido, but his destiny called him to Italy. Heartbroken, Dido cursed Aeneas and his descendants before taking her own life.

keyMyths.title: The War in Italy
keyMyths.description: Aeneas arrived in Italy and faced war with Turnus, king of the Rutulians, who was betrothed to Lavinia, the daughter of King Latinus. After a fierce battle, Aeneas defeated and killed Turnus, securing his place in Italy.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Epic poem detailing Aeneas's journey from Troy to Italy and his role in the founding of Rome.
sources.text: Epic poem detailing Aeneas's journey from Troy to Italy and his role in the founding of Rome.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Mentions Aeneas as a Trojan hero and a member of the royal family.
sources.text: Mentions Aeneas as a Trojan hero and a member of the royal family.
